statement.en: Impact and change in decision-making and job roles in organisations adopting AI/Automation solutions for the first time
statement.summary: AI and Automation are not new topics; the technologies, systems and processes within this area have been rapidly gaining momentum in the last few years. Research suggests that the interest in AI/Automation peaked in 2005 and then waned. However, post-2015, the interest in AI/Automation has increased significantly. Industries such as manufacturing, and construction embraced automation more than two decades ago to increase quality and productivity. In recent years, better technology solutions related to storage, big data management and data interpretation have led to AI/Automation being applied to decision-making across industries and job roles. Based on research in this thesis, job roles will evolve with the use of both AI and Automation in organisations to execute repetitive tasks more eff

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The axiom describes an empirical claim about organizational change dynamics in real-time (2015-2026). It is not yet a validated theorem—the work appears to be an incomplete thesis abstract (text cuts off mid-sentence). The claim about job role evolution is provisional, under active research validation, and subject to ongoing organizational and technological change. No refutation exists, but formalization is incomplete.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 役割は反復性が消えるほど、判断の複雑さが増すほど、進化する。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 自動化は古い反復的な労働を吸収し、判断と創造性への新しい流路を開く。
